task_completion.visual_evidence: The Zotero main window is visible on the screen.
A window titled 'Import' or a file browser dialog for selecting the file to import is visible over Zotero.

task_completion.description: The correct RIS file is selected for import
task_completion.visual_evidence: The file 'machine_learning_papers.ris' in the /home/ga/Documents/ directory is highlighted in the file picker or displayed in the import wizard.

task_completion.description: A collection named 'Machine Learning Papers' is created
task_completion.visual_evidence: The left sidebar in Zotero shows a folder/collection specifically named 'Machine Learning Papers'.

task_completion.description: The imported bibliographic items are present inside the 'Machine Learning Papers' collection
task_completion.visual_evidence: With the 'Machine Learning Papers' collection selected in the left sidebar, the middle pane shows the imported machine learning papers (e.g., titles like 'Deep learning', 'Attention Is All You Need', 'Generative adversarial nets'). There should be exactly 8 items in total.

integrity.description: The agent used the Zotero graphical interface to perform the import and collection creation.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking through Zotero menus (e.g., File -> Import) and dialogs, rather than using terminal commands to modify the database.

integrity.description: The application shown is genuinely Zotero.
integrity.visual_evidence: The standard Zotero UI elements (My Library, middle pane for items, right pane for metadata) are clearly visible.

integrity.description: The items were imported from the file rather than manually typed.
integrity.visual_evidence: Evidence of the import dialogs being used, and the items appearing with complete metadata all at once, rather than the agent opening the 'New Item' dialog and typing details manually.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires importing an RIS file located at /home/ga/Documents/machine_learning_papers.ris. The file contains exactly 8 items, which are well-known machine learning papers (e.g., by LeCun, Krizhevsky, Goodfellow, Vaswani, Silver, Brown, Devlin, He). The final Zotero collection must be named 'Machine Learning Papers' (case-insensitive) and contain these 8 items.
